name: journald
explanation: journald is a logs-only integration: there is no Agent metric check (no `journald.py`), only a logs configuration template. The Agent's logs subsystem reads the systemd journal locally via `/var/log/journal` or `/run/log/journal`. There is no network endpoint, no host/port, no authentication. The integration's required input is the operator's intent — which units to include/exclude and what `source`/`service` tags to apply — which is policy, not a discoverable attribute. Discovery would also have to verify that the Agent runs on a host with systemd-journald and that `dd-agent` is in the `systemd-journal` group, both of which are out-of-band setup steps. Closest existing patterns: pattern N (DogStatsD/instrumentation-only — non-probe integration) and pattern J (user supplies the configuration template).
notes: Logs-only host-local integration; no AgentCheck class. Same structural class as sidekiq (no probe target), but driven by the Agent's logs collector reading the journal rather than DogStatsD.
